Now, when we talk about this idea of communication, I want to just delve deeper into just for one second here and explain this more to communication than actually meets the eye with the words that we say. In fact, when it comes down to it, the words are only a sliver of human communication. In human communication, there are three aspects. You have your tonality, which makes up about 40% of human communication, or 50% depending on what study you buy into. Then you have your body language, which makes up 40% or 50% depending on what study you buy into. And then the last 9%, which is pretty much is constant, is the actual words that you say. The words only make up 9% of human communication. Now, let me just stop for one second here. Let's not say that the words aren't absolutely critical, because when you're talking, when you're actually speaking those words, they matter 100%. The point is you're communicating very often without even speaking. When you're speaking, the words do matter, but you so often are communicating on an unconscious level through tonality, through body language. And when all three of those are wrapped up in unison at the right level and being executed correctly, bam, then you're really making headway. And that's how you get yourself boom into that instant and that powerful, that must have rapport. I don't want you to get the wrong idea about your words, that you can say things that don't make sense or just go off the reservation and talk about things that are not germane to the sale you're making. I call that going off into Pluto, where you're not showing the person that you're really on the ball in the sale. You can't say things that don't make sense. You see, despite this being only 9% of communication, when you're actually speaking, it matters 100%. You understand what I'm saying here? It's not that it only malia. My words only are 9% important when I'm saying them. No, no, no, no, no. Your words are absolutely crucial while you're saying them. And that is why we learn how to use these special sort of scripts that I'm going to teach you about in a later module that actually show you what to say, exactly when to say it, how to say it, and so on and so forth. See, if you're out there saying things that don't make sense, you break rapport with people and yeah, you can get back into it, but obviously you make it a whole lot harder on yourself if the things that you say are simply not making sense. So my point is this, when I say that 9% of your communication is words, what I'm saying is that 91% of the time, you're communicating without speaking. But when you do actually open your mouth, you want to make sure that gems come out. The words that come out need to make sure they feed this bigger idea that I care about the prospect and you should if you're doing things right and ethically and be that on some level, you're just like them, you're relatable and you're in rapport and bam, moving down the straight line. Okay. Thanks. Bye. Bye. Bye.
